csv.message: Add the URL of your CSV file to be ingested. This will work for any web-hosted CSV file. For example, You can create a file in google sheets following the format at this <anchor>link</anchor> and then construct the CSV URL by publishing your google sheet in the CSV format.
defineRecipe.docsHint: For more information about how to configure a recipe, see the <anchor>{{displayName}} source docs.</anchor>
defineRecipe.lookerBanner.acknowledge: You must acknowledge this message to proceed!
defineRecipe.lookerBanner.acknowledgeBut: I have set up LookML ingestion!
defineRecipe.lookerBanner.integration: To get complete Looker metadata integration (including Looker views and lineage to the underlying warehouse tables), you must <bold>also</bold> use the <anchor>DataHub lookml module</anchor>.
defineRecipe.lookerBanner.uiUnsupported: LookML ingestion <bold>cannot</bold> currently be performed via UI-based ingestion. This is a known problem the DataHub team is working to solve!
defineRecipe.title: Configure {{displayName}} Recipe
docHint.guide: Check out the <anchor>{{displayName}} Guide</anchor> to understand the prerequisites, learn about available settings, and view examples to help connect to the data source.
docHint.intro: To import from {{displayName}}, we'll need some more information to connect to your instance.
docHint.title: Let's get connected! 🎉
fields.columnProfiling.tooltip: Run column-level profiling in addition to table stats. Substantially increases time and cost. Requires Table Profiling enabled.
fields.databasePatternAllow.helper: Regex patterns to include specific databases (e.g. prod_.*).
fields.databasePatternAllow.tooltip: Only include specific Databases by providing the name of a Database, or a Regular Expression (REGEX). If not provided, all Databases will be included.
fields.databasePatternDeny.helper: Regex patterns to exclude databases. Deny overrides allow.
fields.databasePatternDeny.tooltip: Exclude specific Databases by providing the name of a Database, or a Regular Expression (REGEX). If not provided, all Databases will be included. Deny patterns always take precedence over Allow patterns.
fields.enableColumnProfiling.tooltip: Generate Data Profiles for the Columns in extracted Tables. Enabling this may increase the duration of the sync.
fields.enableStatefulIngestion.tooltip: Remove stale assets from DataHub once they have been deleted in the ingestion source.
fields.enableTableProfiling.tooltip: Generate Data Profiles for extracted Tables. Enabling this may increase the duration of the sync.
fields.env.tooltip: Optional environment label (e.g. PROD/DEV/STG). Defaults to PROD.
fields.extractOwners.tooltip: When enabled, extracts ownership from Looker directly. When disabled, ownership is left empty for dashboards and charts.
fields.extractUsageHistory.tooltip: Experimental (Subject to breaking change) -- Whether to ingest usage statistics for dashboards. Setting this to True will query looker system activity explores to fetch historical dashboard usage.
fields.gitInfoRepo.helper: URL or name of Git repository
fields.includeLineage.tooltip: Include Table and View lineage in your ingestion.
fields.includeTableLineage.tooltip: Extract Table-Level lineage metadata. Enabling this may increase the duration of the sync.
fields.ingestOwner.tooltip: Ingest Owner from source. Be careful: This can override Owners added by users of DataHub.
fields.ingestTags.tooltip: Ingest Tags from the source. Be careful: This can override Tags entered by users of DataHub.
fields.removeStaleMetadata.tooltip: Automatically remove deleted tables from DataHub. Maintains catalog accuracy when Glue tables are dropped.
fields.schemaPatternAllow.tooltip: Only include specific Schemas by providing the name of a Schema, or a Regular Expression (REGEX) to include specific Schemas. If not provided, all Schemas inside allowed Databases will be included.
fields.schemaPatternDeny.tooltip: Exclude specific Schemas by providing the name of a Schema, or a Regular Expression (REGEX). If not provided, all Schemas inside allowed Databases will be included. Deny patterns always take precedence over Allow patterns.
fields.skipPersonalFolders.tooltip: Whether to skip ingestion of dashboards in personal folders. Setting this to True will only ingest dashboards in the Shared folder space.
fields.startTime.tooltip: Earliest date used when processing audit logs for lineage, usage, and more. Default: Last full day in UTC or last time DataHub ingested usage (if stateful ingestion is enabled). Tip: Set this to an older date (e.g. 1 month ago) to bootstrap your first ingestion run, and then reduce for subsequent runs. Changing this may increase the duration of the sync.
fields.tablePatternAllow.tooltip: Only include Tables with particular names by providing the fully qualified name of a Table, or a Regular Expression (REGEX). If not provided, all Tables inside allowed Databases and Schemas will be included in ingestion.
fields.tablePatternDeny.tooltip: Exclude Tables with particular names by providing the fully qualified name of a Table, or a Regular Expression (REGEX). If not provided, all Tables inside allowed Databases and Schemas will be included in ingestion. Deny patterns always take precedence over Allow patterns.
fields.tableProfiling.tooltip: Run profiling queries for table statistics.
fields.viewPatternAllow.tooltip: Only include Views with particular names by providing the fully qualified name of a View, or a Regular Expression (REGEX). If not provided, all Views inside allowed Databases and Schemas will be included in ingestion.
fields.viewPatternDeny.tooltip: Exclude Views with particular names by providing the fully qualified name of a View, or a Regular Expression (REGEX). If not provided, all Views inside allowed Databases and Schemas will be included in ingestion. Deny patterns always take precedence over Allow patterns.
looker.warning: To complete the Looker integration (including Looker views and lineage to the underlying warehouse tables), you must <bold>also</bold> use the <anchor></anchor>.
multiStep.builder.discard.description: Exiting now will discard your configuration. You can continue setup or exit and start over later
multiStep.builder.discard.title: You have unsaved changes
multiStep.connection.cannotChangeSourceT: It's not possible to change source type for existing ingestion source
multiStep.connection.cliVersion.help: Advanced: Provide a custom CLI version to use for ingestion.

multiStep.connection.debugMode.help: Advanced: Turn on debug mode in order to get more verbose logs.
multiStep.connection.debugMode.label: Debug Mode
multiStep.connection.executorId.help: Provide the ID of the executor that should execute this ingestion recipe. This ID is used to route execution requests of the recipe to the executor of the same ID. The built-in DataHub executor ID is 'default'. Do not change this unless you have configured a custom executor via actions framework.
multiStep.connection.executorId.label: Executor ID

multiStep.connection.sourceName.label: Source Name

multiStep.education.cli: Prefer to keep credentials local? Get started with <anchor>DataHub's Ingestion CLI</anchor>
multiStep.education.heading: Set Up Your First Source in Minutes
multiStep.education.intro: We'll guide you through each step - Ask DataHub is here to help with configuration and troubleshooting as you go.
multiStep.education.lineage: Then, connect upstream platforms where data originates or downstream tools where it's consumed, and let DataHub automatically map lineage between them.
multiStep.education.proTip: <label>Pro Tip: </label>Start with your data warehouse (Snowflake, BigQuery, Databricks) as a strong foundation.
multiStep.education.title: Connect Your Data
multiStep.filters.description: Optional. Leave blank to ingest all accessible assets. Create include rules to allow specific assets, exclude rules to block them, or both.
multiStep.filters.title: Asset Filters
multiStep.filters.tooltip.intro: Use fully qualified names based on your filter type.
multiStep.recipeForm.connectionDetailsDe: Configure how DataHub connects to {{sourceDisplayName}}. <securityText><securityLink>Learn more about keeping credentials in your environment.</securityLink></securityText>
